title: Boat Rental in Paris Seine: A 2026 Guide to Cruising the Seine
html:
Exploring Paris Seine by boat offers a unique perspective on the city's landmarks, and with BoatHire24 you can find a vessel for any budget, from EUR 60 for an hour on a shared tour to EUR 2,500 for a full-day crewed yacht, departing from Port de la Bourdonnais near the Eiffel Tower.

Where to set off in Paris Seine

Boating in Paris Seine begins at one of the city's public ports. Port de la Bourdonnais sits at the foot of the Eiffel Tower and is ideal for hire a boat on the Seine. Port de l'Arsenal in the 12th arrondissement connects to the Canal Saint-Martin. Port de Javel-Haut near the Statue of Liberty replica offers direct access to the river. Port de Bercy, once a wine depot, now serves as a marina. Port de Suffren and Port des Champs-Élysées complete the main options, each with easy metro connections. You can hire a boat in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Paris Seine?

Boat rental prices in Paris Seine vary from EUR 60 per person for a shared tour to over EUR 2,000 for a private luxury yacht for the day. Costs depend on boat size, duration, crew, and season. Below is a typical price-band breakdown. One option worth a look is the Princess F70 for hire.

  • Shared tour (electric boat): about EUR 60 per person for one hour
  • Small motorboat (self-drive): from EUR 150 for a half-day, no licence required for slower boats
  • Catamaran (skippered): from EUR 800 for three hours, sleeping up to 12 guests
  • Luxury yacht (crewed): from EUR 2,500 for a full-day charter, including catering

All prices are in EUR and subject to seasonal change. For a high-end experience, consider the Princess F70 for hire on BoatHire24.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (Axopar 22): best for quick sightseeing trips; up to 6 guests; from EUR 180 for half-day
  2. Sloop or catamaran (Lagoon 40): ideal for a relaxing cruise with friends; up to 10 adults; from EUR 500 for four hours
  3. Luxury motor yacht (Princess F70): for a premium dinner cruise; up to 12 guests with crew; from EUR 2,000 for four hours
  4. Open launch (Antaris 9.0): a classic option for a romantic evening; up to 8 guests; from EUR 250 for two hours

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Paris Seine?

For most small, slow boats (under 15 knots, under 10 metres) you do not need a licence; the operator will give a safety briefing. For larger or faster vessels a valid boating licence (e.g., French permis côtier or equivalent) is required. Always confirm exact requirements with the boat owner on BoatHire24 before booking. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

Pass beneath over 30 bridges between the Eiffel Tower and Notre-Dame; the best light for photographs comes at sunset from around late June to early August.

To understand the full process, review how boat rental works on BoatHire24, covering booking, deposits, and cancellation policies. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

Seasons and weather in Paris Seine

The high season in Paris Seine runs from May to September, with July and August being the warmest and busiest. In summer, the prevailing westerly wind can create light chop on the river but rarely cancels trips. Spring and autumn offer milder weather and fewer crowds, though temperatures drop from October. Winter navigation is possible but less popular; many boats are laid up from November to March.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 60, 2,500
  • Peak months: July to August
  • Minimum trip length: 1 hour shared; 2 hours private
  • Max capacity: 12 guests (up to 30 on some tour boats)
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Paris Seine

Can I cruise all the way to the sea from Paris?

The Seine is navigable from Paris to the English Channel, but a trip from central Paris to Le Havre covers about 200 nautical miles and requires several days and, for larger boats, a licence.

Are there toilets on board rental boats in Paris Seine?

Most cabin cruisers and yachts include a toilet; small open boats usually do not, but you can use marina facilities along the river.

What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my booking?

Licensed skippers may cancel for safety; on self-drive rentals you are responsible for assessing conditions, and some operators offer a refund or reschedule.

How to book your boat in Paris Seine

BoatHire24's platform connects you with boat owners directly: you browse available vessels, check availability, and book securely online. Prices include insurance, and you pay only the advertised amount. If you own a boat in Paris Seine, you can also rent out your boat through BoatHire24's become-a-host service, earning extra income from idle days.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Paris Seine and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
